description_long: This street food stall consists of a long, rectangular counter made of concrete and wood, topped with a variety of kitchenware and food containers. The countertop is densely arranged with pots, pans, trays, plates, spice jars, and serving dishes, suggesting a fully equipped setup for preparing and serving food. The surface is segmented into different work areas, including sections for cooking, plating, and storing utensils. The stall features a sturdy concrete base with visible support bars, providing stability and durability for heavy use. The materials and arrangement evoke a practical, utilitarian design suitable for high-traffic urban environments. The overall appearance is clean and organized, with a focus on functionality and efficient service.
description: A long, concrete and wood street food stall with a fully equipped countertop featuring pots, trays, plates, and utensils. The setup is practical, organized, and designed for efficient food preparation and service.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the stall, with all kitchenware and containers clearly visible and neatly arranged across the countertop.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the counter and the alignment of pots, trays, and plates along the length of the stall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the stall, with the arrangement of kitchenware and the concrete support structure more prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other end of the counter, showing the distribution of plates and utensils and the overall width of the stall.
attribute_serving_counter: A long, rectangular counter made of concrete and wood, with a flat surface for serving and preparing food, and no visible glass shield or cash register.
attribute_food_display: Food and utensils are displayed openly on the countertop in trays, pots, and plates, allowing easy visibility and access but with no protective covers.
